preamble: Extreme raising of [ε] to the high-front vowel [i] is documented in specific phonetic environments, such as before nasal consonants in certain English and Ancient Greek dialects.

examples.note: Pin-pen merger: [ɛ] raises to [ɪ] before nasal consonants in Southern and some Midland US English.
Ancient Greek ε raised to ι before nasals in Arcado-Cypriot dialect group.
